title: Vriesea montana
taxonomy: Vriesea montana is a member of the family Bromeliaceae, commonly known as the pineapple family. This family consists of over 57 genera and more than 3,000 species of flowering plants. Vriesea montana is specifically classified under the subfamily Tillandsioideae and the genus Vriesea. The genus Vriesea itself is a group of tropical epiphytes native to Central and South America. These epiphytes are characterized by their rosettes of leaves and showy inflorescences.

intro: Vriesea montana is a species of air plant, commonly known as Tillandsia, belonging to the Bromeliad family. The health benefits of Vriesea montana include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which may contribute to its use in treating various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Vriesea montana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
